task_completion.description: Create a top-level collection named 'NeurIPS Preparation'
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the left sidebar of the Zotero window, there should be a folder/collection named exactly 'NeurIPS Preparation'.

task_completion.description: Add all papers published in 'Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems' to the collection
task_completion.visual_evidence: When the 'NeurIPS Preparation' collection is selected, the middle pane should display exactly the 4 target papers: 'Attention Is All You Need', 'Language Models are Few-Shot Learners', 'ImageNet Classification with Deep Convolutional Neural Networks', and 'Generative Adversarial Nets'.

task_completion.description: Tag each of the target papers with 'neurips-reading'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The tag 'neurips-reading' should be visible in the bottom-left tag selector pane, and/or visible in the right pane's 'Tags' tab when one of the target papers is selected. Colored tag indicators might also appear next to the items in the middle pane.

task_completion.description: Create a standalone note titled 'Presentation Outline' within the collection
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the middle pane, within the 'NeurIPS Preparation' collection, there should be a standalone note item (typically indicated by a yellow sticky-note icon) with the title 'Presentation Outline'.

task_completion.description: The note must contain a brief outline (minimum 150 characters) referencing at least two of the target paper titles
task_completion.visual_evidence: When the 'Presentation Outline' note is selected, the right pane should display its contents. The text must be visibly substantial (at least a few sentences/bullet points to meet the ~150 character minimum) and explicitly mention the titles of at least two of the four target papers.

integrity.description: The agent used the Zotero GUI to perform the tasks
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Zotero application interface being actively used (e.g., menus open, items selected, text being typed in the note editor) rather than terminal commands modifying the Zotero SQLite database directly.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Zotero application
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface shown must be the standard Zotero desktop application, not a text editor, web browser, or mock interface.

integrity.description: The note content is an actual outline, not just copy-pasted instructions
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in the 'Presentation Outline' note should look like a generated outline or summary, not a direct copy-paste of the task description prompt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target papers published in 'Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems' are exactly 4: 'Attention Is All You Need', 'Language Models are Few-Shot Learners', 'ImageNet Classification with Deep Convolutional Neural Networks', and 'Generative Adversarial Nets'. The collection must be named 'NeurIPS Preparation'. The tag must be 'neurips-reading'. The note must be titled 'Presentation Outline' and contain >= 150 characters referencing at least two of these specific paper titles.
